text:
There was a quiet country town where the sun always seemed to shine a little brighter. In the middle of its charming cobblestone streets lived an old man named Harold. Harold knew every leaf on every oak tree and could identify birds by their song.

One quiet afternoon, Harold was tending to his pet hummingbird collection when something unusual happened. A small cloud floated overhead, glowing a soft green light. It slowly descended and landed right on his window.

The hummingbirds took off fluttering around their new friend. Harold smiled warmly. Maybe there really was something magical in that cloud.

Over time, Harold learned to communicate with the birds. He’d tell them silly stories about himself, and they’d respond with melodies instead of words. Together they’d have spirited morning dances.

Harold didn’t expect visitors. One day a curious squirrel knocked on his window. It sat there, eyes wide with interest.

“What’s your name?”
